name: ec_seed_18_104_corner_turn
notes: electronic-circuit @ rate=15 from ores. Captured from the growth loop's idx=5 attempt (first 3-tile-tall region) at seed (18,104). 5 iron-plate trunks cross east-flowing copper-cable tap, and a 6th trunk at x=23 corner-turns east out of the zone at (23,103) — same tile has BOTH a South IN boundary (feeder from (23,102) South) AND an East OUT boundary, forcing a belt curve. Previously UNSAT because `encode_perimeter_boundary` ran once per boundary and clashed on `out_dir` (AMO) and the OUT side's upstream-source clause. Fixed by grouping boundaries per tile and dispatching corner-turn tiles to `encode_corner_boundary`, which pins the tile as a surface belt facing d_out with the IN side's external feeder implicitly sourcing it. Optimal cost is 58: 5 trunk UG-in (25) + 5 trunk UG-out (25) + 7 tap belts (7) + 1 corner belt (1). max_cost guards against regression.
